summary: First iteration testing of GadgetsHub (React+FastAPI+MongoDB). Backend is 100% green: all 23 pytest cases pass covering auth (register/login/me/logout, dup email 409, wrong password 401, missing token 401), gadgets list/filters/sort/search/single/404, compare (with order preservation + max 4 enforcement), wishlist (auth-protected add/get/remove/ids). 40 gadgets seeded across 5 categories. Frontend mostly works: home, browse (search/category filter/sort), gadget detail, auth modal (register/login/persist on reload/logout), wishlist server-sync (logged-in path) all working. ONE blocking UI bug found in CompareContext: an item already in the tray cannot be cleared — clicking 'tray-clear-btn' or trying to add items from another category fails to mutate state, leaving stale data and preventing the side-by-side compare table (no '.winning-spec' cells) from ever rendering with multiple items.

ui_bugs.issue: tray-clear-btn does not actually clear compare state — visually the tray hides briefly but item (e.g. MacBook Pro 16 M3 Max) re-appears, blocking subsequent same-category additions. Same-category guard then silently rejects new clicks (toast 'You're comparing laptops. Clear compare to switch.'). End result: /compare page only ever shows 1 item and 0 .winning-spec cells. Reproducible across full reload + localStorage.clear().

integration_issues.issue: Cannot get >1 gadget into tray when there is residual state from a different category. /compare page never reaches winning-spec highlighting state.

action_items: Fix CompareContext.clear() / clearCompare so it actually persists empty array to its storage and prevents re-hydration with stale data on next render. Likely cause: hydration useEffect re-reads localStorage AFTER state is cleared, overwriting it; ensure write-on-change effect only runs after hydration, and clear() also wipes localStorage key directly.

Verify same-category enforcement: when current tray is non-empty and user clicks compare-toggle on a different-category gadget, ensure the toast is shown but consider auto-suggesting clear; current behavior is silent rejection that confuses users.
Consider adding data-testid='compare-table' to ComparePage table for easier QA targeting.
Optional: include winning-spec aria/visual indicator (currently relies on class only).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: bcrypt.checkpw inside verify_password swallows ALL exceptions and returns False — fine for malformed hashes but log the cause for ops visibility.

server.py login/register: always sets cookie via samesite='none' + secure=True. With CORS allow_credentials=False the cookie path is effectively unused; harmless but dead code. Documented as intentional in code comments — OK.
Auth: bcrypt hash format starts with $2b$ (verified indirectly via login round-trip).
seed_admin: idempotent admin upsert + password sync from .env when changed — good.
Seed gadgets idempotent only when count == 0; if seed list grows in future, existing DBs won't pick up new entries. Consider upsert by (name,brand) for future-proofing.
GadgetCard / CompareTray persistence: confirm CompareContext storage key + hydration order (likely the source of the clear-not-persisting bug).
test_credentials: Admin: admin@gadgetshub.com / Admin@123 (verified working). Test users created on demand via /api/auth/register, e.g. test{ts}@gh.com / TestPass123.
seed_data_creation: None created beyond what backend startup seeds (40 gadgets + admin). Several test_*@gh.com users created during testing — safe to leave or purge.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully green — re-run /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (23 tests, ~5s) to regression-check after any backend change. Frontend testid map: AuthModal uses auth-input-name/email/password (not auth-name-input etc.); Navbar uses nav-signup-btn / nav-login-btn / nav-user-btn / nav-logout-btn / wishlist-badge / compare-badge; CompareTray uses tray-clear-btn / tray-go-compare-btn / tray-remove-{id}. Public URL: https://craft-station-7.preview.emergentagent.com (NOT the URL in review_request). For compare retest: must verify clear actually empties state across page reloads and that >=2 same-category items render winning-spec cells on /compare.
rca of the issue: CompareContext clear/state-persistence bug: After page reload + localStorage.clear() from devtools, the tray re-appeared with a previously selected gadget (MacBook Pro 16 M3 Max). Clicking the visible 'tray-clear-btn' makes the tray briefly disappear but state is restored on next render, and same-category guard then blocks all further additions. Likely root cause: in CompareContext, the localStorage→state hydration useEffect runs on every state change (instead of only mount), so when clear() sets state to [], the hydration effect immediately re-reads stale storage and overwrites. Mitigation: split into two effects — one mount-only hydration, one write-on-change persistence — and ensure clear() also explicitly removes the localStorage key. Reproduction: (1) add any gadget to compare, (2) navigate to /compare, (3) try to click 'CLEAR ALL' or tray-clear-btn — observe item returns; (4) navigate to /browse and try to add a different-category gadget — silently rejected with bottom toast.
